message:
chore(beads): review-pass-8 — 4 HIGH fuzz-target gap beads

Applied /testing-fuzzing skill Rule 3 ("every pub fn accepting
untrusted input MUST have a fuzz target") + the Decision Tree
archetype audit. Baseline steady (3171 passed / 0 failed / 13
ignored). Existing fuzz corpus: 22 targets.

  frankenpandas-jkhg [HIGH] fuzz: 3 of 5 fp-expr string-input
                            pub fns lack fuzz targets. Existing
                            fuzz_dataframe_eval covers eval_str
                            only. parse_expr (lowest-level parser
                            — skill Rule 2 "fuzz the parser, not
                            the application"), query_str (boolean-
                            filter path, different from eval),
                            query_str_with_locals (locals-
                            propagation path) all uncovered.
                            Proposes 3 new harnesses + 5-seed
                            corpus each, plugging into existing
                            fuzz/Cargo.toml.

  frankenpandas-gpxk [HIGH] fuzz: 6 pub SQL-reading fns in fp-io
                            lack fuzz targets. read_sql +
                            read_sql_with_options + read_sql_with_index_col
                            + read_sql_table + read_sql_table_with_index_col
                            + read_sql_table_columns all flow
                            untrusted query strings directly
                            into rusqlite's C-interop. Proposes
                            Phase-1 crash detector + Phase-2
                            SQLsmith grammar-based + Phase-3
                            stateful session fuzz + Phase-4
                            differential vs pandas once br-fd90
                            PostgreSQL backend lands.

  frankenpandas-wr9n [HIGH] fuzz: no STATEFUL fuzz target. All
                            22 existing targets are single-op;
                            state-corruption bugs that only
                            manifest across op sequences (e.g.
                            set_index_multi silently dropping
                            row_multiindex, subsequent reset_index
                            fabricates labels) are invisible to
                            current corpus. Proposes
                            fuzz_dataframe_op_chain harness with
                            an op-alphabet (SELECT / DROP_COL /
                            FILTER / GROUPBY / MERGE / SORT /
                            RESET_IX / SET_IX / STACK / UNSTACK /
                            ROUND_TRIP_CSV) + cross-op invariant
                            assertions + Phase-2 shadow-model
                            differential.

  frankenpandas-bahw [HIGH] fuzz: no concurrency / TSan campaign.
                            DataFrame Send + Sync contracts are
                            compile-time verified but not runtime-
                            verified. Rayon par_iter over columns
                            (which the README ROADMAP plans) has
                            no fuzz coverage. br-fd90's tokio-
                            postgres backend (async, Send + !Sync)
                            + br-diic's py.allow_threads escape
                            hatch both require Rust-side thread
                            safety that nothing in the suite
                            presently verifies. Proposes
                            fuzz_parallel_dataframe harness +
                            dedicated TSan CI job (skill Rule 6:
                            separate sanitizer campaigns).

Interlock with prior passes:
- jkhg (expr fuzz) + liai (differential vs pandas fuzz) — natural
  pair: local no-panic fuzz + cross-impl oracle fuzz.
- gpxk (SQL fuzz) + fd90 (SQL backend epic) — fd90 slice 7 lands
  per-backend live oracle; this bead lands structure-aware grammar
  fuzz against the same backends.
- wr9n (stateful fuzz) + k05t (scale fixtures) + 1zzp.5 (row-MI
  conformance) — chain fuzz at scale exercises state-machine
  bugs the tier-S fixtures miss.
- bahw (concurrency) + gztr (sanitizer config) + zjme (fuzz CI) —
  concurrency fuzz is the largest slice of gztr's sanitizer work
  + needs zjme's CI integration.

Running session totals after 8 review passes:
  2 CRITICAL
  29 HIGH
  4 MEDIUM
  1 LOW
= 36 review beads total.

Each bead spells out archetype, harness sketch, seed corpus,
expected exec/s, sanitizer requirements per skill's Checklist,
and interlock commentary with prior beads. Zero shipped-code
bugs. Workspace stays 3171/0/13 throughout all 8 passes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-b9rh","title":"fp-frame DataFrame.iterrows (label, row Series) parity — pd.DataFrame.iterrows()","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T11:22:09.807819965Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T11:22:48.241090009Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T11:22:48.232237010Z","close_reason":"Already implemented at fp-frame/src/lib.rs:17449 (DataFrame::iterrows returns Vec<(IndexLabel, Vec<(&str, Scalar)>)>)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-bahw","title":"[HIGH] fuzz: no concurrency / TSan campaign — DataFrame Send+Sync contracts + parallel iteration safety unverified","description":"Applying /testing-fuzzing skill decision tree: "Concurrency code → TSan campaign".

**Observation.** The existing fuzz corpus runs single-threaded. No target has a `thread::spawn`, no `rayon::par_iter`, no cross-thread DataFrame reference. skill's Sanitizer Discipline table: "TSan if target has concurrency" — we have concurrency latent in the type system but no TSan campaign.

**What's concurrency-relevant in fp-frame despite single-threaded core logic:**

1. **Send + Sync contracts.** DataFrame, Series, Column, Index, MultiIndex all DERIVE Clone (some auto-derive Send/Sync via Vec<Scalar>). Users legitimately pass frames across thread boundaries (`thread::spawn(move || df.clone().sum())`). If any internal field has interior mutability that the compiler auto-disables Send/Sync for — good, compile error. If any `Arc<Mutex<...>>` internal state exists — we need TSan to verify it behaves across parallel iteration. Cargo tree suggests: bumpalo (single-threaded arena), arrow (Send+Sync-assuming), rusqlite (Connection: !Send by default).

2. **Parallel iteration via downstream consumers.** README advertises "Parallel execution (rayon) | Not started; architecture supports it (columns are independent)". But the PUBLIC API already exposes `&Column`, `&Series`, `.values()` — a user writing `rayon::par_iter` over a DataFrame's columns must trust our data types don't share interior-mutable state between columns. Unverified by our test suite.

3. **future: br-fd90 SQL backends.** tokio-postgres is async; `Connection: Send + !Sync` (typical async-client shape). The SqlConnection trait will need careful Send/Sync bounds.

4. **future: br-diic PyO3 bindings.** Python's GIL wraps, but once `py.allow_threads(|| df.sum())` pattern lands (the standard PyO3 escape hatch for compute-heavy calls), Rust-side thread safety becomes load-bearing.

**Skill rule invoked:** Pattern 7 (Concurrency archetype) + Sanitizer Discipline ("TSan campaign for concurrent code"). Current test suite's entire concurrency coverage is `#[test] fn send_sync_traits_auto_derive() {}` — implicit compile-time check, no runtime verification.

**Severity.** HIGH — concurrency bugs are (a) the hardest to reproduce, (b) often silent for months until a hot production path exposes them, (c) a common cause of "seemed fine in tests, corrupted data in prod" disasters. For a dataframe library targeting pandas parity + Rust performance, concurrency safety is part of the implicit contract.

**Fix shape:**

1. `fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_parallel_dataframe.rs` — wraps `fp_conformance::fuzz_parallel_dataframe_bytes`.

2. `fp-conformance::fuzz_parallel_dataframe_bytes(data: &[u8]) -> Result<(), String>`:
   - Project bytes into a frame via `fuzz_*_frame_from_bytes` (existing helper).
   - Clone the frame. Spawn 4-8 rayon par_iter jobs over clones, each running a different op:
       thread 1: sum_across_columns(&clone)
       thread 2: series.rolling(5).mean()
       thread 3: groupby + count
       thread 4: merge with a second frame
   - Join all threads. Assert each thread's result matches the same op run single-threaded on the original frame.
   - Invariant: arithmetic on &DataFrame must be pure (no shared-state mutation between threads).

3. CI setup for TSan:
   - `RUSTFLAGS="-Zsanitizer=thread" cargo +nightly fuzz run fuzz_parallel_dataframe` — separate build (TSan incompatible with ASan).
   - Optional: add a scheduled nightly CI job for TSan runs (skill's Rule 6: separate sanitizer campaigns).

4. Shadow-model concurrency assertion (Phase 2):
   - Use `loom` for exhaustive concurrency exploration of critical fast paths (e.g. groupby's HashMap-based accumulator if it ever goes parallel).
   - Today this is overkill — but file a follow-up bead once br-fd90 async SQL lands and we have genuine concurrency code to verify.

Seed corpus:
- empty frame + 1 thread
- 1-row frame + 2 threads (likely-safe smoke test)
- 100-row frame + 8 threads (stress the rayon work-stealing path)
- frame with a Categorical column + parallel groupby (categorical metadata must not race)

Expected cost: TSan is 5-15x slowdown per skill's sanitizer table. Fuzz_parallel_dataframe runs at maybe 50-100 exec/s (well below the 1000/s parser floor, but WITHIN the 100/s stateful floor which this effectively is).

**Related:**
- br-zjme (fuzz CI) — sanitizer campaigns need their own CI job config (one per sanitizer, shared corpus per skill Rule 6).
- br-gztr (fuzz dictionaries + sanitizers) — this bead is the concurrency slice of gztr's "configure sanitizers" item.
- br-fd90 slice 1/7 (SqlConnection trait) — Send/Sync bounds on the trait need fuzz-verification.
- br-diic (PyO3 bindings) — `py.allow_threads(|| ...)` requires Rust-side thread safety; this bead lights up the path.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:50:49.184997284Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:50:49.184997284Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","fuzz","review","tsan"]}

 {"id":"frankenpandas-gnjt","title":"fp-columnar ValidityMask.slice/concat parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:24:54.280108818Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T08:28:28.173581351Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T08:28:28.171747578Z","close_reason":"Implemented ValidityMask::slice/concat in commit 29b76f1, 3 tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-gpxk","title":"[HIGH] fuzz: 6 pub SQL-reading fns in fp-io lack fuzz targets — crafted query strings hit driver unsafety undetected","description":"Applying /testing-fuzzing skill decision tree: "Database engine → STATEFUL + GRAMMAR-BASED".

**Observation.** fp-io exposes SQL surfaces that accept query strings:

  read_sql<C: SqlConnection>(conn, query: &str)            → fp-io/src/lib.rs:3302
  read_sql_with_options<C: SqlConnection>(conn, ..., options) → fp-io/src/lib.rs:3309
  read_sql_with_index_col                                  → fp-io/src/lib.rs:3360
  read_sql_table<C: SqlConnection>(conn, table_name: &str) → fp-io/src/lib.rs:3429
  read_sql_table_with_index_col                           → fp-io/src/lib.rs:3375
  read_sql_table_columns                                   → fp-io/src/lib.rs:3448

(Note: The SqlConnection trait already exists — br-frankenpandas-fd90's slice 1 or a parallel agent's work on that epic has landed. Still, the strings that flow to the backend are untrusted user input.)

None of these six pub fns have any fuzz target. Input strings flow directly into rusqlite / (future: tokio-postgres / mysql_async) prepared-statement handling. A crafted query could:
- Panic the driver on an unterminated statement.
- OOM via an expression that allocates an enormous result buffer.
- Bypass our layer's own validation to trigger a backend-side bug.
- Hit the column-name → Scalar::* translator with an unexpected SQLite type (e.g. BLOB, NULL with PRAGMA type_affinity off) and crash the downstream DataFrame builder.

**Skill rule invoked:** Rule 3 (fuzz every untrusted-input pub fn) + the "Database engine" branch of the decision tree. The decision-tree recommendation is STATEFUL + GRAMMAR-BASED: generate random-but-valid SQL, run it against a shadow model (an in-memory SQLite with known state).

**Severity.** HIGH — SQL is the highest-risk IO path we expose. Even with `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` on our side, the backend drivers (rusqlite's C-interop for SQLite, future PG/MySQL libs) are unsafe-rich code. A crafted query that exercises an untested path can trigger backend-level UB; our layer's coverage of that path determines whether we detect it or silently forward the result.

**Fix shape:**

Phase 1 — minimum viable fuzz target (crash detector):
1. `fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_sql_read.rs` — wraps `fp_conformance::fuzz_read_sql_bytes`.
2. `fp-conformance::fuzz_read_sql_bytes(data: &[u8]) -> Result<(), String>`:
   - Set up an in-memory rusqlite::Connection with a fixed schema (say, 3 tables: `t1(a INT, b TEXT)`, `t2(k TEXT PRIMARY KEY, v REAL)`, `empty_t()`) populated with 10-20 deterministic rows.
   - Convert data bytes to UTF-8 lossy, clamp to 4096 chars.
   - Call `read_sql(&conn, &query_str)`. Expect Ok or Err; assert no panic.
   - If Ok, assert the returned DataFrame's column count matches the query's SELECT list arity (via a cheap regex on the query string — only for statements that parse as SELECT).

Phase 2 — structure-aware grammar fuzz:
3. Use `SQLsmith` or a small hand-grammar that generates syntactically valid SQL against the fixture schema. Feeds the grammar's output to the same fuzz_read_sql_bytes harness. Catches deeper code paths (JOIN, GROUP BY, subquery, UNION, window functions) that random bytes would never produce.

Phase 3 — STATEFUL:
4. `fuzz_sql_session_bytes` — byte input parsed as a sequence of SQL statements (CREATE, INSERT, UPDATE, SELECT, DELETE). Run each against a rusqlite connection + a shadow BTreeMap model. After each statement, assert the connection's state equals the shadow model's state. Detects state-corruption bugs that single-query fuzz misses.

5. Eventually (once br-fd90 PostgreSQL backend lands): differential fuzz — same grammar-generated SQL against rusqlite AND tokio-postgres, assert same result DataFrame. Catches backend-specific divergence.

Seed corpus (5 minimum for Phase 1):
- empty string
- `SELECT 1` (trivial valid)
- `SELECT * FROM t1` (schema-referencing)
- `SELECT * FROM nonexistent_table` (error path)
- `DROP TABLE t1; SELECT * FROM t1` (multi-statement — likely rejected, assert no panic)

**Related:**
- br-fd90 (SQL backend epic) — slice 7 (live-oracle conformance per backend) naturally overlaps with Phase 2 grammar fuzz.
- br-zjme (fuzz CI) — adds new targets to the existing regression gate.
- br-liai (differential vs pandas) — pandas's `pd.read_sql(query, engine)` can serve as oracle for Phase 2 fuzz.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:49:05.843007879Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:49:05.843007879Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fp-io","fuzz","review","sql"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-gsq6","title":"fp-index Index.asof (nearest-preceding lookup) parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:49:22.296852910Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T08:58:10.959292697Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T08:58:10.957556580Z","close_reason":"Implemented Index::asof in commit b17e94a, 2 tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-gwa2","title":"fp-frame: DataFrame.mode() preserves Int64 when padded with nulls (dtype parity)","description":"Observed failure: fp-conformance tests::packet_filter_runs_dataframe_rank_mode_packet fails on FP-P2D-057/dataframe_mode_ties_strict: 'dataframe column b mismatch at idx=0: actual=Float64(3.0), expected=Int64(3)'.

Fixture: columns a=[1,1,2,2] (2 tied modes) and b=[3,3,3,4] (1 mode). max_modes=2, so column b gets padded with Null(NaN) to match. Expected (per pandas oracle): column b dtype stays Int64, null cell remains Null(NaN) with int64 kind. Actual: fp-frame widens to Float64.

Root: crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:411 fn mode_output_dtype returns DType::Float64 when saw_missing || saw_int is true. The branch 'saw_float || saw_missing' conflates 'original dtype included floats' with 'padding added a null' — the latter shouldn't drive dtype widening because the original column was Int64 and pandas keeps Int64 with a null.

Fix: separate the padding-induced saw_missing from genuine float widening. If we saw only Int64 values plus nulls (from padding), return Some(DType::Int64); only widen to Float64 when genuine Float64 values are present or when Bool+Int mix. Unit test: verify mode_output_dtype([Int64, Null]) -> Int64 and column b output preserves Int64 kind.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T04:42:35.315784398Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:57:10.497455446Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T04:57:10.495528834Z","close_reason":"shipped b544717: mode_axis1 pre-promotes Int->Float when any selected source column is Float64. Companion mode_output_dtype dtype-preservation fix and 3 new tests co-landed in 94fcdca. Verified packet_filter_runs_dataframe_rank_mode_packet now passes; 9/9 df_mode tests green; fp-frame clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-gwga","title":"Add Series.pct_change limit parameter parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-20T18:14:15.410174104Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T18:16:10.748815695Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T18:16:10.745626675Z","close_reason":"Implemented pct_change_with_fill supporting ffill/bfill/pad/backfill + limit with 5 unit tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenpandas-jj8w","title":"pandas: DataFrame.pivot_table median/std/var aggfunc parity","description":"py signature:

df.pivot_table(values="v", index="i", columns="c", aggfunc="std")

Current fp-frame pivot_table_with_dropna in crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs only accepts sum/mean/count/min/max/first. Add the common pandas numeric aggfuncs median/std/var with null-handling and focused parity tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T16:56:14.409738624Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:25:25.267214387Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:25:25.265351301Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-jjj2","title":"fp-columnar Column.unique parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:35:44.029574096Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T06:38:29.458910587Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T06:38:29.456967257Z","close_reason":"Implemented Column::unique in commit a8b0f55, 2 tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-jk7v","title":"Add dataframe head/tail zero-n strict conformance packets","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T00:35:53.824224416Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T00:40:17.213133039Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T00:40:17.211037262Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-jkhg","title":"[HIGH] fuzz: 3 of 5 fp-expr string-input pub fns lack fuzz targets (query_str, query_str_with_locals, parse_expr)","description":"Applying /testing-fuzzing skill Rule 3: "Every pub fn accepting &[u8], &str, impl Read from untrusted sources MUST have a fuzz target."

**Observation.** fp-expr exposes FOUR distinct string-accepting public APIs:

  fp-expr/src/lib.rs:314: pub fn eval_str(s, df, policy, ledger) -> Result<Series>
  fp-expr/src/lib.rs:323: pub fn eval_str_with_locals(s, df, locals, policy, ledger) -> Result<Series>
  fp-expr/src/lib.rs:338: pub fn query_str(s, df, policy, ledger) -> Result<DataFrame>
  fp-expr/src/lib.rs:347: pub fn query_str_with_locals(s, df, locals, policy, ledger) -> Result<DataFrame>
  fp-expr/src/lib.rs:737: pub fn parse_expr(s) -> Result<Expr>

The existing `fuzz_dataframe_eval` target (fuzz_dataframe_eval_bytes at fp-conformance/src/lib.rs:5431) covers `eval_str` only. The other three pub fns have zero fuzz coverage despite accepting untrusted expression strings with identical-or-worse attack surface:

1. `query_str` produces a DataFrame (filter semantics) rather than a Series. Different code path post-parse: the result must survive a boolean-mask projection + index preservation. Bugs there don't surface via eval_str.
2. `parse_expr` is the lowest level — it returns an AST `Expr` directly. If parse_expr panics or infinite-loops on a crafted string, EVERY higher-level eval/query caller inherits the bug. It's the narrowest fuzz target per skill Rule 2 ("fuzz the parser, not the application").
3. `query_str_with_locals` extends query_str with a BTreeMap<String, Scalar> locals argument. The locals-propagation path has its own opportunity for bugs — e.g. a locals key that happens to match a frame column name has precedence semantics that could be tested only via fuzz.

**Skill rule violated:** Rule 3 (every pub fn in the untrusted-input class must have a fuzz target). Today: 4 of 5 such expr pub fns lack fuzz coverage.

**Severity.** HIGH — expression evaluation is a security-sensitive surface. pandas' `df.query("col > 5")` has a documented history of allowing broader expression constructs than users realize (pandas's `numexpr` has had CVEs over eval of arbitrary attribute access). Our surface is smaller but nothing proves absence of infinite loops or panics on crafted strings.

**Fix shape:**

1. `fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_parse_expr.rs` — minimal wrapper around `fp_conformance::fuzz_parse_expr_bytes`.
2. `fp-conformance::fuzz_parse_expr_bytes(data: &[u8]) -> Result<(), String>`:
   - Convert data to lossy UTF-8, clamp to 256 chars.
   - Call parse_expr(&s). Assert no panic. No AST shape invariant (parse_expr returns Err freely).
   - Size guard: `if data.len() > 4096 { return; }`.
3. `fuzz_query_str_bytes(data: &[u8])`:
   - Same input projection.
   - Build a tiny frame via `fuzz_series_from_bytes("x", data)` (reuse existing helper).
   - Call `query_str(&s, &frame, &RuntimePolicy::strict(), &mut EvidenceLedger::new())`.
   - Assert no panic. If Ok, assert `result.index().len() <= frame.index().len()` (query can only shrink).
4. `fuzz_query_str_with_locals_bytes`:
   - Same as query_str_bytes but constructs a 3-key locals BTreeMap from the input bytes (first byte picks the key name from ["x", "col", "__secret__"], value picks from Scalar::Int64(0), Int64(1), Utf8("test"), Null(Null)).
   - Asserts locals propagation doesn't panic.

Seed corpus (5 minimum per target):
- empty string
- syntactically valid trivial: "1"
- common: "col1 > 5"
- boundary: deeply nested "(((((((((x)))))))))" — tests parser recursion depth
- adversarial: Unicode with zero-width joiner + invalid UTF-8 tail

Each target adds to fuzz/Cargo.toml as [[bin]]. Target exec/s: skill's parser floor is 1000/s. These should hit it easily.

**Related:**
- br-zjme (fuzz CI) — these 3 new targets plug into the same CI gate once zjme lands.
- br-liai (differential vs pandas) — eval/query differential fuzz is the natural follow-up: same input → pandas `df.eval()` / `df.query()` → assert semantic_eq with our result.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:48:19.803925628Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:48:19.803925628Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fp-expr","fuzz","pub-api","review"]}

 {"id":"frankenpandas-wl2d","title":"fp-index Index.memory_usage (bytes) parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:49:22.611772105Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T08:58:11.324597322Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T08:58:11.322816711Z","close_reason":"Implemented Index::memory_usage in commit b17e94a, 2 tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-wr9n","title":"[HIGH] fuzz: no STATEFUL fuzz target — 22 existing targets are all single-op; operation-chain state bugs uncovered","description":"Applying /testing-fuzzing skill decision tree: "Protocol with state machine → STATEFUL (sequence of operations with shadow model oracle)".

**Observation.** All 22 existing fuzz targets are SINGLE-OPERATION fuzzers: each takes a byte input, projects it into ONE frame + ONE op, asserts invariants, exits. Examples:

  fuzz_groupby_sum_bytes       — one groupby(.).sum() call
  fuzz_rolling_window_bytes    — one series.rolling(w, mp).agg() call
  fuzz_series_add_bytes        — one left.add(&right)
  fuzz_format_cross_round_trip — one primary->secondary format hop

None exercises SEQUENCES of operations. In real pandas usage, users chain 3-10 ops per pipeline:
  df.query("col > 0").groupby("key").agg_named(...).reset_index().merge(other, ...).sort_values(...)

**Why that matters.** DataFrame / Series carry state that propagates through chained ops:
- `index` carries labels + optional row_multiindex (post br-1zzp.1)
- `column_multiindex` survives column-reshape ops
- `ValidityMask` propagates through arithmetic
- Column-order (Vec<String>) is preserved by some ops, reordered by others
- Categorical metadata should survive groupby but NOT arithmetic (per README)

A state-corruption bug in op N that's only observable after op N+2 or N+3 is invisible to the current single-op fuzz corpus. Concrete example: if `set_index_multi(&cols)` silently drops a row_multiindex from the result (regression of br-1zzp.1), a subsequent `reset_index()` fabricates the missing labels rather than pulling them from the MultiIndex. Single-op fuzz tests each call; chain fuzz would catch the silent drop.

**Skill rule invoked:** The skill's Decision Tree explicitly routes "Protocol with state machine" to STATEFUL archetype (Pattern 4 in HARNESS-CATALOG.md). Our DataFrame is effectively a state machine: index, columns, validity masks, multiindex metadata, categorical dtypes all evolve across operations.

**Severity.** HIGH — state-corruption bugs are the class most resistant to targeted unit tests (because the "bug" is an interaction, not a single-op violation) and most likely to surface as user-visible divergences from pandas. The current fuzz corpus has 0% coverage of the state-machine dimension.

**Fix shape:**

1. `fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_dataframe_op_chain.rs` — wraps `fp_conformance::fuzz_dataframe_op_chain_bytes`.

2. `fp-conformance::fuzz_dataframe_op_chain_bytes(input: &[u8]) -> Result<(), String>`:
   - Bytes [0..N]: initial frame projection (N = ~32 bytes, produces small frame via existing fuzz_*_frame_from_bytes helpers).
   - Byte N: op count (clamp to 2..=8).
   - Bytes N+1..: per-op bytes, one byte tag picks an op from a fixed op alphabet:
       OP_SELECT   — df.select(&[col])
       OP_DROP_COL — df.drop_column(col)
       OP_FILTER   — df.query("col < const")
       OP_GROUPBY  — df.groupby(&[col]).sum()
       OP_MERGE    — df.merge(other_fixture_frame, how=inner)
       OP_SORT     — df.sort_values(col)
       OP_RESET_IX — df.reset_index()
       OP_SET_IX   — df.set_index(col) / set_index_multi(&[c1, c2])
       OP_STACK    — df.stack()
       OP_UNSTACK  — df.unstack()
       OP_ROUND_TRIP_CSV  — df.write_csv_string + read_csv_str
       (initial op alphabet: 12 ops; expandable)

   - Apply each op to the running frame. If any op returns Err, stop the chain without flagging — some op sequences are genuinely invalid.
   - After the chain: assert cross-op invariants:
     * `frame.index().len() == frame.columns.values().map(|c| c.len()).max()` (all columns aligned to the index)
     * `frame.column_order().len() == frame.columns().len()` (no orphan columns)
     * If row_multiindex is Some, its height matches index length (br-1zzp.1 invariant).
     * If any column's dtype is Categorical, the codes vector length matches the index length.

3. Shadow model variant (Phase 2): build a minimal reference implementation of the op alphabet using `BTreeMap<String, Vec<Scalar>>` (the most naive DataFrame). Run the chain against both the real DataFrame and the shadow. Assert same output frame → semantic_eq. Catches divergence bugs we couldn't otherwise distinguish from "this op sequence is invalid."

Seed corpus:
- empty (no ops)
- Single op: `OP_GROUPBY` then stop
- Classic chain: `OP_FILTER → OP_GROUPBY → OP_RESET_IX`
- Stress: 8-op chain exercising `OP_STACK → OP_UNSTACK → OP_SET_IX → OP_RESET_IX` round-trips (should be identity)

Expected exec/s: skill's stateful floor is 100/s. Chain depth 8 × per-op ~0.1ms = ~800µs per case. Well above floor.

**Related:**
- br-liai (differential fuzz vs pandas) — natural extension: run the same op chain through pandas, assert cross-impl semantic_eq. That's a separate target in the `liai` family, not duplicative.
- br-zjme (fuzz CI) — new target plugs into the CI regression run.
- br-k05t (scale fixtures) — stateful chain fuzz at size tier M (1k-10k rows) catches scale-specific state bugs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:49:58.607149104Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:49:58.607149104Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["archetype","fuzz","review","stateful"]}
